before:
defmodule ConceptWeb.PageEditorTest do
  use ConceptWeb.ConnCase, async: false
  import Phoenix.LiveViewTest

  alias Concept.Accounts
  alias Concept.Pages
  alias Concept.Repo
  import Ecto.Query

  setup %{conn: conn} do
    {:ok, user} =
      Concept.Accounts.User
      |> Ash.Changeset.for_create(:register_with_password, %{
        email: "palette#{System.unique_integer([:positive])}@example.com",
        password: "passw0rd!",
        password_confirmation: "passw0rd!"
      })
      |> Ash.create(authorize?: false)

    # Confirm user directly
    Repo.update_all(
      from(u in Concept.Accounts.User, where: u.id == ^user.id),
      set: [confirmed_at: DateTime.utc_now()]
    )

    # Sign in to get token
    {:ok, signed_in} =
      Concept.Accounts.User
      |> Ash.Query.for_read(:sign_in_with_password, %{email: user.email, password: "passw0rd!"})
      |> Ash.read_one(authorize?: false)

    conn =
      conn
      |> Phoenix.ConnTest.init_test_session(%{})
      |> Plug.Conn.put_session("user_token", signed_in.__metadata__.token)

    {:ok, [ws]} = Accounts.Workspace.for_user(user.id, actor: user)
    {:ok, page} = Pages.create_page("Roadmap", ws.id, nil, actor: user, tenant: ws.id)
    _ = Pages.create_page("Meeting Notes", ws.id, nil, actor: user, tenant: ws.id)

    {:ok, conn: conn, user: user, ws: ws, page: page}
  end

  # ── BUG-001 ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  test "block-handle renders per text block", %{conn: conn, ws: ws, page: page, user: user} do
    {:ok, _block} =
      Pages.create_block(page.id, :paragraph, ws.id, nil, actor: user, tenant: ws.id)

    {:ok, view, _html} = live(conn, ~p"/w/#{ws.slug}/p/#{page.id}")

    assert has_element?(view, "ora-block-handle")

    {:ok, _block2} =
      Pages.create_block(page.id, :paragraph, ws.id, nil, actor: user, tenant: ws.id)

    {:ok, view2, _html} = live(conn, ~p"/w/#{ws.slug}/p/#{page.id}")

    handles = LazyHTML.query(LazyHTML.from_fragment(render(view2)), "ora-block-handle")
    assert Enum.count(handles) == 2
  end

  test "format toolbar renders exactly once on page", %{
    conn: conn,
    ws: ws,
    page: page,
    user: user
  } do
    # 0 blocks
    {:ok, view, _html} = live(conn, ~p"/w/#{ws.slug}/p/#{page.id}")
    toolbars = LazyHTML.query(LazyHTML.from_fragment(render(view)), "ora-format-toolbar")
    assert Enum.count(toolbars) == 1

    # 1 block
    {:ok, _} = Pages.create_block(page.id, :paragraph, ws.id, nil, actor: user, tenant: ws.id)
    {:ok, view2, _html} = live(conn, ~p"/w/#{ws.slug}/p/#{page.id}")
    toolbars2 = LazyHTML.query(LazyHTML.from_fragment(render(view2)), "ora-format-toolbar")
    assert Enum.count(toolbars2) == 1

    # 2 blocks
    {:ok, _} = Pages.create_block(page.id, :paragraph, ws.id, nil, actor: user, tenant: ws.id)
    {:ok, view3, _html} = live(conn, ~p"/w/#{ws.slug}/p/#{page.id}")
    toolbars3 = LazyHTML.query(LazyHTML.from_fragment(render(view3)), "ora-format-toolbar")
    assert Enum.count(toolbars3) == 1
  end

  test "handle row layout class present", %{conn: conn, ws: ws, page: page, user: user} do
    {:ok, _} = Pages.create_block(page.id, :paragraph, ws.id, nil, actor: user, tenant: ws.id)
    {:ok, view, _html} = live(conn, ~p"/w/#{ws.slug}/p/#{page.id}")

    assert has_element?(view, ".ora-block-row")
  end

  # ── BUG-002 ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  test "insert_paragraph_below creates new block at tail", %{
    conn: conn,
    ws: ws,
    page: page,
    user: user
  } do
    {:ok, block} = Pages.create_block(page.id, :paragraph, ws.id, nil, actor: user, tenant: ws.id)
    {:ok, view, _html} = live(conn, ~p"/w/#{ws.slug}/p/#{page.id}")

    view
    |> find_live_child("page-editor-#{page.id}")
    |> render_hook("insert_paragraph_below", %{"block_id" => block.id})

    {:ok, blocks} = Pages.list_for_page(page.id, actor: user, tenant: ws.id)
    assert length(blocks) == 2
    new_block = Enum.find(blocks, &(&1.id != block.id))
    assert new_block.position > block.position

    new_block_id = new_block.id
    assert_push_event(view, "focus_block_caret", %{block_id: new_block_id, position: "start"})
  end

  test "insert_paragraph_below between siblings", %{conn: conn, ws: ws, page: page, user: user} do
    {:ok, block_a} =
      Pages.create_block(page.id, :paragraph, ws.id, nil, actor: user, tenant: ws.id)

    {:ok, block_b} =
      Pages.create_block(page.id, :paragraph, ws.id, nil, actor: user, tenant: ws.id)

    {:ok, view, _html} = live(conn, ~p"/w/#{ws.slug}/p/#{page.id}")

    view
    |> find_live_child("page-editor-#{page.id}")
    |> render_hook("insert_paragraph_below", %{"block_id" => block_a.id})

    {:ok, blocks} = Pages.list_for_page(page.id, actor: user, tenant: ws.id)
    assert length(blocks) == 3
    new_block = Enum.find(blocks, &(&1.id != block_a.id && &1.id != block_b.id))
    assert new_block.position > block_a.position
    assert new_block.position < block_b.position
  end

  test "nav_block down focuses next block", %{conn: conn, ws: ws, page: page, user: user} do
    {:ok, block1} =
      Pages.create_block(page.id, :paragraph, ws.id, nil, actor: user, tenant: ws.id)

    {:ok, block2} =
      Pages.create_block(page.id, :paragraph, ws.id, nil, actor: user, tenant: ws.id)

    {:ok, view, _html} = live(conn, ~p"/w/#{ws.slug}/p/#{page.id}")

    block2_id = block2.id

    view
    |> find_live_child("page-editor-#{page.id}")
    |> render_hook("nav_block", %{"direction" => "down", "block_id" => block1.id})

    assert_push_event(view, "focus_block_caret", %{block_id: block2_id, position: "start"})
  end

  test "nav_block up focuses previous block", %{conn: conn, ws: ws, page: page, user: user} do
    {:ok, block1} =
      Pages.create_block(page.id, :paragraph, ws.id, nil, actor: user, tenant: ws.id)

    {:ok, block2} =
      Pages.create_block(page.id, :paragraph, ws.id, nil, actor: user, tenant: ws.id)

    {:ok, view, _html} = live(conn, ~p"/w/#{ws.slug}/p/#{page.id}")

    block1_id = block1.id

    view
    |> find_live_child("page-editor-#{page.id}")
    |> render_hook("nav_block", %{"direction" => "up", "block_id" => block2.id})

    assert_push_event(view, "focus_block_caret", %{block_id: block1_id, position: "end"})
  end

  test "nav_block up at first block is no-op", %{conn: conn, ws: ws, page: page, user: user} do
    {:ok, block1} =
      Pages.create_block(page.id, :paragraph, ws.id, nil, actor: user, tenant: ws.id)

    {:ok, view, _html} = live(conn, ~p"/w/#{ws.slug}/p/#{page.id}")

    view
    |> find_live_child("page-editor-#{page.id}")
    |> render_hook("nav_block", %{"direction" => "up", "block_id" => block1.id})

    refute_push_event(view, "focus_block_caret", %{}, 100)
  end

  test "delete_block_merge archives empty block and focuses previous", %{
    conn: conn,
    ws: ws,
    page: page,
    user: user
  } do
    {:ok, block1} =
      Pages.create_block(page.id, :paragraph, ws.id, nil, actor: user, tenant: ws.id)

    {:ok, block2} =
      Pages.create_block(page.id, :paragraph, ws.id, nil, actor: user, tenant: ws.id)

    {:ok, view, _html} = live(conn, ~p"/w/#{ws.slug}/p/#{page.id}")

    view
    |> find_live_child("page-editor-#{page.id}")
    |> render_hook("delete_block_merge", %{"block_id" => block2.id})

    {:ok, blocks} = Pages.list_for_page(page.id, actor: user, tenant: ws.id)
    assert length(blocks) == 1

    block1_id = block1.id
    assert_push_event(view, "focus_block_caret", %{block_id: block1_id, position: "end"})
  end

  test "delete_block_merge on only block is no-op", %{conn: conn, ws: ws, page: page, user: user} do
    {:ok, block} = Pages.create_block(page.id, :paragraph, ws.id, nil, actor: user, tenant: ws.id)
    {:ok, view, _html} = live(conn, ~p"/w/#{ws.slug}/p/#{page.id}")

    view
    |> find_live_child("page-editor-#{page.id}")
    |> render_hook("delete_block_merge", %{"block_id" => block.id})

    {:ok, blocks} = Pages.list_for_page(page.id, actor: user, tenant: ws.id)
    assert length(blocks) == 1
  end

  test "block_created does not produce duplicates", %{conn: conn, ws: ws, page: page, user: user} do
    {:ok, view, _html} = live(conn, ~p"/w/#{ws.slug}/p/#{page.id}")

    view
    |> find_live_child("page-editor-#{page.id}")
    |> render_hook("add_first_block", %{})

    {:ok, blocks} = Pages.list_for_page(page.id, actor: user, tenant: ws.id)
    assert length(blocks) == 1

    doc = LazyHTML.from_fragment(render(view))
    block_els = LazyHTML.query(doc, "ora-block")
    assert Enum.count(block_els) == 1
  end

  # ── BUG-018 ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  test "reorder_block moves block to first position", %{
    conn: conn,
    ws: ws,
    page: page,
    user: user
  } do
    {:ok, b1} =
      Pages.create_block(page.id, :paragraph, ws.id, nil, actor: user, tenant: ws.id)

    {:ok, b2} =
      Pages.create_block(page.id, :paragraph, ws.id, nil, actor: user, tenant: ws.id)

    {:ok, b3} =
      Pages.create_block(page.id, :paragraph, ws.id, nil, actor: user, tenant: ws.id)

    {:ok, view, _html} = live(conn, ~p"/w/#{ws.slug}/p/#{page.id}")

    editor_view = find_live_child(view, "page-editor-#{page.id}")

    # Move b3 to first position: prev_id = nil, next_id = b1.id
    render_hook(editor_view, "reorder_block", %{
      "block_id" => b3.id,
      "prev_id" => nil,
      "next_id" => b1.id
    })

    {:ok, blocks} = Pages.list_for_page(page.id, actor: user, tenant: ws.id)
    ids = Enum.map(blocks, & &1.id)
    assert ids == [b3.id, b1.id, b2.id]
  end

  test "reorder_block moves block to last position", %{
    conn: conn,
    ws: ws,
    page: page,
    user: user
  } do
    {:ok, b1} =
      Pages.create_block(page.id, :paragraph, ws.id, nil, actor: user, tenant: ws.id)

    {:ok, b2} =
      Pages.create_block(page.id, :paragraph, ws.id, nil, actor: user, tenant: ws.id)

    {:ok, b3} =
      Pages.create_block(page.id, :paragraph, ws.id, nil, actor: user, tenant: ws.id)

    {:ok, view, _html} = live(conn, ~p"/w/#{ws.slug}/p/#{page.id}")

    editor_view = find_live_child(view, "page-editor-#{page.id}")

    # Move b1 to last position: prev_id = b3.id, next_id = nil
    render_hook(editor_view, "reorder_block", %{
      "block_id" => b1.id,
      "prev_id" => b3.id,
      "next_id" => nil
    })

    {:ok, blocks} = Pages.list_for_page(page.id, actor: user, tenant: ws.id)
    ids = Enum.map(blocks, & &1.id)
    assert ids == [b2.id, b3.id, b1.id]
  end

  test "reorder_block moves block to middle position", %{
    conn: conn,
    ws: ws,
    page: page,
    user: user
  } do
    {:ok, b1} =
      Pages.create_block(page.id, :paragraph, ws.id, nil, actor: user, tenant: ws.id)

    {:ok, b2} =
      Pages.create_block(page.id, :paragraph, ws.id, nil, actor: user, tenant: ws.id)

    {:ok, b3} =
      Pages.create_block(page.id, :paragraph, ws.id, nil, actor: user, tenant: ws.id)

    {:ok, view, _html} = live(conn, ~p"/w/#{ws.slug}/p/#{page.id}")

    editor_view = find_live_child(view, "page-editor-#{page.id}")

    # Move b3 between b1 and b2: prev_id = b1.id, next_id = b2.id
    render_hook(editor_view, "reorder_block", %{
      "block_id" => b3.id,
      "prev_id" => b1.id,
      "next_id" => b2.id
    })

    {:ok, blocks} = Pages.list_for_page(page.id, actor: user, tenant: ws.id)
    ids = Enum.map(blocks, & &1.id)
    assert ids == [b1.id, b3.id, b2.id]
  end

  test "reorder_block is no-op when dropped at same position", %{
    conn: conn,
    ws: ws,
    page: page,
    user: user
  } do
    {:ok, b1} =
      Pages.create_block(page.id, :paragraph, ws.id, nil, actor: user, tenant: ws.id)

    {:ok, b2} =
      Pages.create_block(page.id, :paragraph, ws.id, nil, actor: user, tenant: ws.id)

    {:ok, view, _html} = live(conn, ~p"/w/#{ws.slug}/p/#{page.id}")

    editor_view = find_live_child(view, "page-editor-#{page.id}")

    # Move b2 to where it already is: prev_id = b1.id, next_id = nil
    render_hook(editor_view, "reorder_block", %{
      "block_id" => b2.id,
      "prev_id" => b1.id,
      "next_id" => nil
    })

    {:ok, blocks} = Pages.list_for_page(page.id, actor: user, tenant: ws.id)
    ids = Enum.map(blocks, & &1.id)
    assert ids == [b1.id, b2.id]
  end

  test "reorder_block with unknown block_id is safe no-op", %{
    conn: conn,
    ws: ws,
    page: page,
    user: user
  } do
    {:ok, b1} =
      Pages.create_block(page.id, :paragraph, ws.id, nil, actor: user, tenant: ws.id)

    {:ok, view, _html} = live(conn, ~p"/w/#{ws.slug}/p/#{page.id}")

    editor_view = find_live_child(view, "page-editor-#{page.id}")

    render_hook(editor_view, "reorder_block", %{
      "block_id" => "nonexistent",
      "prev_id" => nil,
      "next_id" => nil
    })

    {:ok, blocks} = Pages.list_for_page(page.id, actor: user, tenant: ws.id)
    assert length(blocks) == 1
  end

  # ── BUG-016 ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  test "link editor host renders alongside format toolbar", %{
    conn: conn,
    ws: ws,
    page: page,
    user: user
  } do
    {:ok, view, _html} = live(conn, ~p"/w/#{ws.slug}/p/#{page.id}")

    assert has_element?(view, "#format-toolbar-host")
    assert has_element?(view, "ora-format-toolbar")
    assert has_element?(view, "ora-link-editor")
  end

  test "bold text in block content renders as <strong>", %{
    conn: conn,
    ws: ws,
    page: page,
    user: user
  } do
    bold_lexical = %{
      "root" => %{
        "type" => "root",
        "children" => [
          %{
            "type" => "paragraph",
            "children" => [
              %{
                "type" => "text",
                "text" => "bold text here",
                "format" => 1,
                "detail" => 0,
                "mode" => "normal",
                "style" => "",
                "version" => 1
              }
            ],
            "direction" => "ltr",
            "format" => "",
            "indent" => 0,
            "version" => 1
          }
        ],
        "direction" => "ltr",
        "format" => "",
        "indent" => 0,
        "version" => 1
      }
    }

    # Pure Lexical→HTML transformation — no DB needed; the block storage path is
    # exercised by other tests, and `update_content` requires holding the lock.
    _ = {conn, ws, page, user}
    html = Concept.Lexical.to_html(bold_lexical)
    assert html =~ "<strong>"
    assert html =~ "bold text here"
  end

  # ── BUG-017 ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  test "insert_block_below with type creates typed block", %{
    conn: conn,
    ws: ws,
    page: page,
    user: user
  } do
    {:ok, block} = Pages.create_block(page.id, :paragraph, ws.id, nil, actor: user, tenant: ws.id)
    {:ok, view, _html} = live(conn, ~p"/w/#{ws.slug}/p/#{page.id}")

    view
    |> find_live_child("page-editor-#{page.id}")
    |> render_hook("insert_block_below", %{"block_id" => block.id, "type" => "heading_1"})

    {:ok, blocks} = Pages.list_for_page(page.id, actor: user, tenant: ws.id)
    assert length(blocks) == 2

    new_block = Enum.find(blocks, &(&1.id != block.id))
    assert new_block.type == :heading_1
    assert new_block.position > block.position

    assert_push_event(view, "focus_block_caret", %{block_id: new_block.id, position: "start"})
  end

  test "insert_block_below backward compat insert_paragraph_below creates paragraph", %{
    conn: conn,
    ws: ws,
    page: page,
    user: user
  } do
    {:ok, block} = Pages.create_block(page.id, :paragraph, ws.id, nil, actor: user, tenant: ws.id)
    {:ok, view, _html} = live(conn, ~p"/w/#{ws.slug}/p/#{page.id}")

    view
    |> find_live_child("page-editor-#{page.id}")
    |> render_hook("insert_paragraph_below", %{"block_id" => block.id})

    {:ok, blocks} = Pages.list_for_page(page.id, actor: user, tenant: ws.id)
    assert length(blocks) == 2

    new_block = Enum.find(blocks, &(&1.id != block.id))
    assert new_block.type == :paragraph
  end
end

defmodule Concept.Pages.BlockTypes do
  @moduledoc """
  Compile-time registry of all `Concept.Pages.BlockType` implementations.
  Add a 15th type by appending to `:concept, :block_types` in `config.exs`.
  """

  @modules Application.compile_env(:concept, :block_types, [])

  defmodule UnknownBlockType do
    defexception [:type]
    def message(%{type: t}), do: "no block type module registered for #{inspect(t)}"
  end

  @doc "All registered BlockType modules in declaration order."
  def all, do: @modules

  @doc "All public-facing block type atoms (excludes :hidden auxiliaries)."
  def all_types, do: Enum.map(@modules, & &1.type())

  @doc "Lookup BlockType module by atom type."
  def lookup(type) do
    Enum.find(@modules, &(&1.type() == type)) || raise UnknownBlockType, type: type
  end

  def lookup_safe(type) do
    case Enum.find(@modules, &(&1.type() == type)) do
      nil -> {:error, :unknown}
      mod -> {:ok, mod}
    end
  end

  @doc """
  Resolve a block type string (from client payload) to a validated atom.
  Returns `{:ok, atom}` on success or `{:error, :unknown_type}` if the
  type is not in the registry.
  """
  def resolve(type_str) when is_binary(type_str) do
    type = String.to_existing_atom(type_str)
    lookup_safe(type)
  rescue
    ArgumentError -> {:error, :unknown_type}
  end

  @doc "Slash-menu items grouped, hiding internal auxiliaries (`group: :hidden`)."
  def slash_menu_items do
    @modules
    |> Enum.map(fn m -> Map.put(m.slash_menu(), :type, m.type()) end)
    |> Enum.reject(&(&1.group == :hidden))
  end
end

ArrowUp → caret in "Hello world" paragraph at end. 12. Select "world" → click B in format toolbar → bolded. 13. Select "Hello" → click link → enter "https://elixir-lang.org" → Enter → linked. 14. Save (auto on blur). Reload the page → 12+13 persist. 15. Drag the handle of the heading block above the paragraph → reorder persists on reload. 16. Cmd-K → palette opens → type page title → Enter → navigates back to the page. 17. Open a second browser tab on same URL → presence avatar appears in PresenceBar in both tabs; renaming title from tab 1 reflects in tab 2 within 2s. 18. In tab 1 focus block A → tab 2 shows colored left-bar on block A. 19. Reviewer agents (read-only) cannot drive a browser; this is why W*.6 uses ~task~. Dispatch runs *in parallel* with W*.5 to compress the critical path.   RAG/GraphRAG over Concept's pages & blocks. Wraps Arcana with
  workspace tenancy + Ash policies.

  use Ash.Domain, otp_app: :concept, extensions: [AshAdmin.Domain]

    resource Concept.Knowledge.Citation do
      define :create_citation, action: :create
      define :citations_for_message, action: :for_message, args: [:message_id]
      define :citations_for_block, action: :for_block, args: [:block_id]
    end
  end
end

before:
defmodule Concept.Knowledge.ProfileDsl do
  @moduledoc """
  Spark DSL extension declaring compile-time retrieval+generation profiles.

  See `Concept.Knowledge.Profiles` for the concrete declarations and
  `Concept.Knowledge.Profile` for the resulting struct shape.

  Profile model strings are validated against the Gemini family regex at
  compile time; opt out for dev by setting `CONCEPT_ALLOW_ANY_LLM=1`.
  """

  @gemini_model_regex ~r/^google:(gemini-[\d.]+(-flash|-pro|-flash-lite)?(-preview)?|gemini-embedding-[12])(-[\w.]+)?$/

  @profile %Spark.Dsl.Entity{
    name: :profile,
    target: Concept.Knowledge.Profile,
    args: [:name],
    describe: "A named retrieval+generation profile.",
    schema: [
      name:        [type: :atom, required: true],
      description: [type: :string, default: ""],
      rewrite:     [type: :boolean, default: false],
      search:      [type: :keyword_list, default: [mode: :hybrid, limit: 10]],
      rerank:      [type: :boolean, default: false],
      answer:      [type: :keyword_list, required: true],
      ground:      [type: :boolean, default: false],
      tools:       [type: {:list, :atom}, default: []],
      loop?:       [type: :boolean, default: false]
    ],
    transform: {__MODULE__, :transform_entity, []}
  }

  @section %Spark.Dsl.Section{
    name: :profiles,
    top_level?: true,
    entities: [@profile]
  }

  use Spark.Dsl.Extension, sections: [@section]

  @doc false
  def transform_entity(%Concept.Knowledge.Profile{} = profile) do
    profile = normalize_struct(profile)

    case profile.answer[:model] do
      nil ->
        {:error, "profile #{inspect(profile.name)}: missing `answer model:`"}

      model ->
        if allowed_model?(model) do
          {:ok, profile}
        else
          {:error,
           "profile #{inspect(profile.name)}: model #{inspect(model)} is not a Gemini-family " <>
             "model. Allowed pattern: #{inspect(Regex.source(@gemini_model_regex))}. " <>
             "Set CONCEPT_ALLOW_ANY_LLM=1 to override (dev only)."}
        end
    end
  end

  # Spark stuffs the entity's positional args into the schema's matching keys, then
  # builds the target struct from the schema. But `rewrite`/`rerank`/`ground`/`loop?` in
  # the schema map to struct keys `rewrite?`/`rerank?`/`ground?`/`loop?` respectively.
  defp normalize_struct(%Concept.Knowledge.Profile{} = p) do
    schema_extras = %{
      rewrite?: Map.get(p, :rewrite, p.rewrite?),
      rerank?:  Map.get(p, :rerank,  p.rerank?),
      ground?:  Map.get(p, :ground,  p.ground?)
    }

    struct(p, schema_extras)
  end

  defp allowed_model?(model) when is_binary(model) do
    System.get_env("CONCEPT_ALLOW_ANY_LLM") == "1" or Regex.match?(@gemini_model_regex, model)
  end

  defp allowed_model?(_), do: false
end

PLAN-001 ("Notion MVP pixel-perfect") and PLAN-002 ("Fix Editor UX") were both marked DONE on 2026-05-05 but a 4-agent frontend audit on 2026-05-16 found the editor broken at multiple JS↔server seams that the original tests did not exercise. In particular: ai_answer block render, chat panel, semantic search merge into palette, ~/admin/arcana~ mount, ~#block-~ deep-link JS handler, ambient citation rail. - Caret column tracking across cross-block ArrowUp/Down (start/end fallback is the contract). - Keyboard shortcuts for formatting (Cmd-B etc.) — separate FEAT-011 subtask, not on the critical path. - Replacing AshAuthentication.Phoenix's LiveView surfaces with bespoke LV. - Composite block row insert/delete after creation; cell merging; column resize — FEAT-050 child-creation only. * Execution principles These are inviolable for every implementer, every reviewer, and the main session itself: 1. *Never* run ~git stash~, ~git checkout ~, ~git reset~, ~git revert~, ~git clean -f~, or any other destructive history/working-tree command. If a change needs to be undone, write a forward-fix commit or use ~edit { kind: undo }~ within the current uncommitted edit window. The branch only moves forward. 2. Each implementer task touches a *disjoint set of files* from every other task in the same wave, *or* declares ~blockers~ to serialize on the shared file. 3. Each implementer task ends with a clean ~mix precommit~ + ~bun test~ / ~npx vitest run~ in =assets/=, with a single semantic commit. Reviewer waves never commit; they only ~report_finding~. 4. Reviewer waves run a *different* subagent than the implementer (~reviewer~ agent type, read-only tooling). Reviewers must include at least one puppeteer interaction check that exercises the user-visible behavior — not just unit tests passing — to catch the PLAN-002 failure class. 5. Failing reviewer → file a follow-up child task on the offending item, do not block the wave; the failed item moves to ~gate_failed~ state. 6. ~mix precommit~ must be green at the close of every wave's reviewer phase. Specific revisions vs the original conservative plan above: - BUG-024 (delete one file) /hoisted to W1/ as quick_task (no conflicts). - W4 and W5 /merged into a single parallel batch/ (BUG-025 quick_task + BUG-027 quick_task + FEAT-050 task) — all disjoint files. - Final reviewer covers cleanup sweep + 20-step E2E in one pass. Net: 5 work waves + 4 review waves + a browser-verification wave after every reviewer (=W*.6=). ** TDD evidence contract (every implementer task) Each implementer subagent must include a *red→green* paragraph in its final report: - /Phase A/: write the failing tests, run them, paste the failing output snippet (the exact assertion mismatch). - /Phase B/: implement, re-run, paste the passing output snippet. Single commit is allowed; the *report* must show both phases. Reviewer waves verify the report contains both phases; missing evidence → ~gate_failed~ on the item. ** Browser verification wave (W*.6) After every code-level reviewer wave (W*.5) and before the next implementer wave, a ~task~ subagent (has full tool access incl. ~puppeteer~) drives the dev server through every user-visible behavior shipped in the preceding wave. Reviewer agents (read-only) cannot drive a browser; this is why W*.6 uses ~task~. Dispatch runs *in parallel* with W*.5 to compress the critical path. Next wave only dispatches when BOTH W*.5 (code) and W*.6 (browser) are green. ** Agent routing per wave - W1: 2 ~task~ (BUG-014, BUG-015) + 1 ~quick_task~ (BUG-024). One dispatch each batch. - W1.5: 1 ~reviewer~. - W2: 3 ~task~ in one batch with intra-batch DAG via ~blockers~ (BUG-018 → BUG-016 → BUG-017). - W2.5: 1 ~reviewer~. - W3: 5 ~task~ in one batch with intra-batch DAG (BUG-019 → BUG-021 chained on app.css; BUG-020 ∥ BUG-022 ∥ BUG-023 parallel). - W3.5: 1 ~reviewer~. - W4: 2 ~quick_task~ (BUG-025, BUG-027) + 1 ~task~ (FEAT-050), dispatched as 2 parallel batches. Full user journey on a fresh DB: 1. Register new user → land on /w/. 2. Sidebar empty → click "+ New page". 3. New page renders with editable title + emoji button. 4. Click title → type "My first page" → blur → sidebar updates. 5. Click emoji → pick 📒 → header updates. 6. Click cover band → pick blue → header gets blue cover. 7. Click "+ Click to add your first block" → paragraph appears. 8. Type "Hello world". 9. Press Enter at end → new empty paragraph below; caret in it. 10. Type "/h1 Title" → wait — typo: type "/h1" actually selects heading_1 → press Enter → heading_1 block appears; typed content. Type "Big title". 11. ArrowUp → caret in "Hello world" paragraph at end. 12. Select "world" → click B in format toolbar → bolded. 13. Select "Hello" → click link → enter "https://elixir-lang.org" → Enter → linked. 14. Save (auto on blur). Reload the page → 12+13 persist. 15. Drag the handle of the heading block above the paragraph → reorder persists on reload. 16. Cmd-K → palette opens → type page title → Enter → navigates back to the page. 17. Open a second browser tab on same URL → presence avatar appears in PresenceBar in both tabs; renaming title from tab 1 reflects in tab 2 within 2s. 18. In tab 1 focus block A → tab 2 shows colored left-bar on block A. 19. Slash → Table → 2×2 picker → table appears with 4 cells; Tab from cell (1,1) focuses (1,2); type in each cell, reload, persists. 20. Sign out → /sign-in renders with .ora-auth-root visible. All 20 steps must pass. Any failure files a child BUG and the wave reports ~gate_failed~. * Verification (overall) - ~mix precommit~ green at every wave close. - ~cd assets && npx vitest run~ green at every wave close. - Reviewer puppeteer flow passes end-to-end at W5.5. - Test count target: +60–80 scenarios across 13 BUGs + 1 FEAT. - No file in =lib/= imports a hook by name string that isn't in =assets/js/hooks/index.js= (orphan-hook regression check, runs in CI). - No CustomEvent dispatched by an =ora-*= component without a listener somewhere in =assets/js/hooks/= (orphan-event regression check, vitest static scan). * Execution principles (recap, normative for every subagent) 1. NEVER ~git stash | checkout | reset | revert | clean -f~. Forward-fix only. 2. Each item touches the file set declared in its node. Reviewers cross-check. 3. Each wave closes with ~mix precommit~ green + a single commit per item. 4. Reviewer waves are read-only; they ~report_finding~ + file follow-ups, never edit. 5. Reviewer subagent ≠ implementer subagent for the same item. 6. Puppeteer is mandatory in every reviewer wave — server tests passing is not sufficient evidence. 7. Items declaring ~blockers~ within a wave serialize cleanly; without ~blockers~ they run in parallel. 8. Failing reviewer → ~gate_failed~ on the item + new child BUG; wave proceeds; next wave gated on overall green at close. * References - [[id:PLAN-001-notion-mvp-pixel-perfect]] — original Notion MVP plan (DONE; overstated) - [[id:PLAN-002-fix-editor-ux-edit-bars-enter-inserts-bl]] — first editor-fix plan (DONE; regressed on JS side) - [[id:PLAN-004-arcana-awareness-let-the-document-be-awa]] — Arcana/RAG plan; explicitly disjoint scope - =assets/js/hooks/SERVER_CONTRACT.md= — canonical event names/payloads; do not rename - AGENTS.md — Phoenix 1.8 / LiveView / HEEx mandates including ~current_scope~ and == contract - Frontend audit transcript: this session, agents 0-EditorBlockUX, 1-ShellPageHeaderNav, 2-PaletteAuthHome, 3-AIArcanaFrontendGaps (2026-05-16)

    = has no ~phx-hook~/~id~, hook never mounts. → [[id:BUG-018-blocklist-sortablejs-hook-unattached-dra]] 5. /BlockKeyboard orphan/ — PLAN-002 said removed; file still present, no longer imported. → [[id:BUG-024-blockkeyboard-hook-is-dead-code-plan-002]] 6. /Auth forms = raw DaisyUI/ — =AuthOverrides= module body is comments only. → [[id:BUG-019-authoverrides-module-is-empty-sign-in-re]] 7. /Onboarded users stranded on /, → [[id:BUG-020-onboarded-user-stranded-on-landing-no-po]] 8. /Lock indicators never rendered/ — CSS hook exists, code path absent. → [[id:BUG-021-block-lock-indicators-not-rendered-from]] 9. /PageHeader stale on remote rename/ — missing ~send_update~. → [[id:BUG-022-pageheader-stale-on-remote-rename-missin]] 10. /Empty title placeholder breaks on whitespace/ — no server trim. → [[id:BUG-023-empty-title-placeholder-broken-on-whites]] 11. /current_scope contract not threaded/ — latent failure surface; AGENTS.md mandate. → [[id:BUG-015-current-scope-contract-not-threaded-thro]] 12. /PageEditor container ~phx_hook~ orphan/ → [[id:BUG-027-pageeditor-container-phx-hook-attribute]] 13. /HomeLive missing Cmd-K/ → [[id:BUG-025-homelive-missing-globalkeys-hook-cmd-k-s]] 14. /Composite blocks (table/columns) child UI deferred indefinitely/ → [[id:FEAT-050-composite-blocks-table-columns-child-cel]] Arcana frontend gaps (FEAT-024..029 in PLAN-003) are explicitly out-of-scope here — they are the subject of [[id:PLAN-004-arcana-awareness-let-the-document-be-awa]]. * Scope ** IN - 13 BUGs (BUG-014, 015, 016, 017, 018, 019, 020, 021, 022, 023, 024, 025, 027) and 1 FEAT (FEAT-050) listed above. - Reviewer subagent verification after every implementer wave. - Full E2E puppeteer smoke at the end. ** OUT - Anything Arcana-related — covered by PLAN-004. In particular: ai_answer block render, chat panel, semantic search merge into palette, ~/admin/arcana~ mount, ~#block-~ deep-link JS handler, ambient citation rail. - Caret column tracking across cross-block ArrowUp/Down (start/end fallback is the contract). - Keyboard shortcuts for formatting (Cmd-B etc.) — separate FEAT-011 subtask, not on the critical path. - Replacing AshAuthentication.Phoenix's LiveView surfaces with bespoke LV. - Composite block row insert/delete after creation; cell merging; column resize — FEAT-050 child-creation only. * Execution principles These are inviolable for every implementer, every reviewer, and the main session itself: 1. *Never* run ~git stash~, ~git checkout ~, ~git reset~, ~git revert~, ~git clean -f~, or any other destructive history/working-tree command. If a change needs to be undone, write a forward-fix commit or use ~edit { kind: undo }~ within the current uncommitted edit window. The branch only moves forward. 2. Each implementer task touches a *disjoint set of files* from every other task in the same wave, *or* declares ~blockers~ to serialize on the shared file. 3. Each implementer task ends with a clean ~mix precommit~ + ~bun test~ / ~npx vitest run~ in =assets/=, with a single semantic commit. Reviewer waves never commit; they only ~report_finding~. 4. Reviewer waves run a *different* subagent than the implementer (~reviewer~ agent type, read-only tooling). Reviewers must include at least one puppeteer interaction check that exercises the user-visible behavior — not just unit tests passing — to catch the PLAN-002 failure class. 5. Failing reviewer → file a follow-up child task on the offending item, do not block the wave; the failed item moves to ~gate_failed~ state. 6. ~mix precommit~ must be green at the close of every wave's reviewer phase. CI is the contract. * Architecture sketch #+begin_src text ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ PageEditorLive │ │ ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │ │
      │ │ │ │
    • │ │ │ │ (drag handle) │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ Lexical editor ─── focusStart/End ─┐ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │
      • ... │ │ │ │ │
    │ │ │ │ │
    │ │ │ │ │ ── toggle-format ──┤ │ │ │ │ ── apply-link ─────┤ │ │ │ │
    │ │ │ │ │
    │ │ │ │ │ ── select-item ─────┤ │ │ │ │
    ▼ │ │ │ └────────── PubSub: workspace::page::* ─────┘ │ │ │ │ │ handle_event: nav_block / insert_block_below / │ │ delete_block_merge / reorder_block / │ │ focus_block (presence_meta) / blur_block / │ │ save_content / save_title │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ▲ │ live_render │ ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ WorkspaceLive │ │ │ │ <.sidebar workspace= pages= current_page= ../> │ │ <.live_component PageHeader id= page= ../> │ │ └── send_update from WorkspaceLive on page_updated │ │ {live_render PageEditorLive ...} │ │ <.live_component CommandPaletteLive ../> │ │ │ │ on_mount: live_user_required + AfterSignIn redirect │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ #+end_src * Wave manifest /Aggressive revision (post-decision)/: each numbered wave dispatches as a single =proxy_task= batch with intra-batch DAG via the ~blockers~ field; reviewer waves dispatch a single ~reviewer~ agent; ~quick_task~ used for strictly mechanical items (single-line edits, file deletion). Specific revisions vs the original conservative plan above: - BUG-024 (delete one file) /hoisted to W1/ as quick_task (no conflicts). - W4 and W5 /merged into a single parallel batch/ (BUG-025 quick_task + BUG-027 quick_task + FEAT-050 task) — all disjoint files. - Final reviewer covers cleanup sweep + 20-step E2E in one pass. Net: 5 work waves + 4 review waves + a browser-verification wave after every reviewer (=W*.6=). ** TDD evidence contract (every implementer task) Each implementer subagent must include a *red→green* paragraph in its final report: - /Phase A/: write the failing tests, run them, paste the failing output snippet (the exact assertion mismatch). - /Phase B/: implement, re-run, paste the passing output snippet. Single commit is allowed; the *report* must show both phases. Reviewer waves verify the report contains both phases; missing evidence → ~gate_failed~ on the item. ** Browser verification wave (W*.6) After every code-level reviewer wave (W*.5) and before the next implementer wave, a ~task~ subagent (has full tool access incl. ~puppeteer~) drives the dev server through every user-visible behavior shipped in the preceding wave. Reviewer agents (read-only) cannot drive a browser; this is why W*.6 uses ~task~. Dispatch runs *in parallel* with W*.5 to compress the critical path. Next wave only dispatches when BOTH W*.5 (code) and W*.6 (browser) are green. ** Specialist reviewer pattern (W*.5) Each W*.5 dispatch is a /parallel batch of focused ~reviewer~ agents/, not one monolith. Each specialist owns a thin slice and reports independently. The batch closes when all specialists return; findings aggregate. | Specialist | Owns | |---|---| | /Contract reviewer/ | =SERVER_CONTRACT.md= ↔ hook dispatches ↔ server ~handle_event~ alignment. Orphan-event + orphan-hook scans. CustomEvent producer/consumer pairing. | | /Test depth reviewer/ | Every claimed test scenario maps to a real assertion. No skipped / pending tests. TDD red→green evidence present in implementer reports. Test files committed alongside impl. | | /Convention reviewer/ | AGENTS.md adherence: Phoenix 1.8 layout contract, HEEx syntax, Ash ~actor + tenant~ pattern, ~current_scope~ threading, LiveView stream idioms, hero-icon usage, ~<.input>~ component, no inline scripts. | | /Scope-drift reviewer/ | Each commit's diff stays within the item's declared ~filesDeps~. No accidental Arcana touches. No drive-by edits. Commit attribution accurate (file delta matches the message). | | /Build & test runner/ | ~mix compile --warnings-as-errors~ + ~mix test~ + ~cd assets && npx vitest run~ + ~mix precommit~. No new warnings. Test count matches claims. | | /Architecture reviewer/ | Implementation matches the architecture sketch in PLAN body. No surprising new modules. No future-Arcana coupling. | Dispatch shape: =proxy_task { agent: "reviewer", tasks: […6 specialist tasks…] }=. Each task has a focused ~assignment~. Aggregated outcome → ~proceed~ / ~fix…~. Browser wave (W*.6) stays a single ~task~ agent (only one needs puppeteer access; doesn't parallelise well across one user journey). ** Agent routing per wave - W1: 2 ~task~ (BUG-014, BUG-015) + 1 ~quick_task~ (BUG-024). One dispatch each batch. - W1.5: 1 ~reviewer~. - W2: 3 ~task~ in one batch with intra-batch DAG via ~blockers~ (BUG-018 → BUG-016 → BUG-017). - W2.5: 1 ~reviewer~. - W3: 5 ~task~ in one batch with intra-batch DAG (BUG-019 → BUG-021 chained on app.css; BUG-020 ∥ BUG-022 ∥ BUG-023 parallel). - W3.5: 1 ~reviewer~. - W4: 2 ~quick_task~ (BUG-025, BUG-027) + 1 ~task~ (FEAT-050), dispatched as 2 parallel batches. Disjoint files. - W4.5: 1 ~reviewer~ — cleanup sweep + 20-step E2E in single pass. ** ITEM Wave 1 — Foundation (parallel; disjoint files) :PROPERTIES: :CUSTOM_ID: PLAN-005-concept-editor-and-shell-rescue-post-pla::wave-1 :END: Pre-requisite contract additions that every subsequent wave depends on. Both items touch *no shared file*. - [[id:BUG-014-orablock-missing-focusstart-focusend-arr]] OraBlock ~focusStart()~ + ~focusEnd()~ + lexical caret helpers - Files: =assets/js/components/ora-block.js=, =assets/js/lexical/commands.js=, =assets/js/hooks/block_editor.js=, =test/concept_web/live/page_editor_test.exs= (new), =assets/js/components/__tests__/ora-block.test.js= (new) - [[id:BUG-015-current-scope-contract-not-threaded-thro]] ~Concept.Accounts.Scope~ struct + ~current_scope~ assign in on_mount chain - Files: =lib/concept/accounts/scope.ex= (new), =lib/concept_web/live_user_auth.ex=, =lib/concept_web/components/layouts.ex=, =lib/concept_web/live/home_live.ex=, =lib/concept_web/live/workspace_live.ex=, =test/concept/accounts/scope_test.exs= (new), =test/concept_web/live/scope_assign_test.exs= (new) ** ITEM Wave 1.5 — Reviewer R1 :PROPERTIES: :CUSTOM_ID: PLAN-005-concept-editor-and-shell-rescue-post-pla::wave-1-review :END: Subagent: ~reviewer~. Read-only. Checks: - ~mix precommit~ green; ~cd assets && npx vitest run~ green. - ~lsp definition~ on ~OraBlock.focusStart~ returns a real implementation (not stub). - ExUnit ~focus_block_caret~ round-trip assertions present + green. - Puppeteer: sign in, open a page, type two paragraph blocks, ArrowUp → caret visibly in first block (assert via ~document.activeElement~). Enter at end → new block, caret in new block. - Puppeteer: any LV renders ==; inspect socket assigns via ~:sys.get_state~ proxied through the page (or use a temporary LV test asserting ~@current_scope~ presence). - Reports any seam between Lit caret API and Lexical state — explicit list of selection-changing commands exercised. ** ITEM Wave 2 — Editor wiring blockers (serial on page_editor_live.ex) :PROPERTIES: :CUSTOM_ID: PLAN-005-concept-editor-and-shell-rescue-post-pla::wave-2 :END: All three add HEEx to =PageEditorLive.render/1=. Serialize via ~blockers~ to avoid edit conflicts on that template, while their hook files / lexical command files / server handlers remain disjoint. - [[id:BUG-018-blocklist-sortablejs-hook-unattached-dra]] Attach BlockList SortableJS hook + ~reorder_block~ server handler + ~Pages.update_block_position~ - Files: =lib/concept_web/live/page_editor_live.ex= (render attrs on existing =
      =), =lib/concept/pages.ex= (new ~update_block_position~), =lib/concept/pages/block.ex= (already accepts :position; verify), =assets/js/hooks/block_list.js= (verify), =assets/js/hooks/__tests__/block_list.test.js= (new), =test/concept_web/live/page_editor_test.exs= (extend). - First in wave; smallest template touch. - [[id:BUG-016-format-toolbar-dispatches-orphan-events]] FormatToolbar hook + Lexical format/link wrappers + render host - Blockers: BUG-018. - Files: =assets/js/lexical/commands.js= (extend with toggleFormat/setLink), =assets/js/hooks/format_toolbar.js= (new), =assets/js/hooks/index.js= (register), =lib/concept_web/live/page_editor_live.ex= (add host element near top of template), =assets/js/hooks/__tests__/format_toolbar.test.js= (new), =test/concept_web/live/page_editor_test.exs= (extend). - [[id:BUG-017-slash-menu-never-mounted-typing-slash-do]] SlashMenu hook + server ~insert_block_below~ extension with type param - Blockers: BUG-016. - Files: =assets/js/hooks/slash_menu.js= (new), =assets/js/hooks/index.js= (register), =lib/concept_web/live/page_editor_live.ex= (handle_event extension + slash menu host), =assets/js/hooks/__tests__/slash_menu.test.js= (new), =test/concept_web/live/page_editor_test.exs= (extend with typed-insert scenarios). ** ITEM Wave 2.5 — Reviewer R2 :PROPERTIES: :CUSTOM_ID: PLAN-005-concept-editor-and-shell-rescue-post-pla::wave-2-review :END: Subagent: ~reviewer~. Checks: - ~mix precommit~ + vitest green. - Puppeteer: open a page with two blocks; drag block A's handle to block B's position; release; assert visual order changed + persists across reload. - Puppeteer: type "hello world" in a paragraph; select "world"; click B in format toolbar; assert =font-weight~ visually rendered; save; reload; assert =world~ in DOM. - Puppeteer: link round-trip — toolbar → ora-link-editor → "https://example.com" → Enter → reload → ~~ in DOM. - Puppeteer: focus a block; type "/"; assert slash menu appears at caret rect; arrow-down twice; press Enter → new block of selected type appears below; caret in it. - LSP: ~grep "phx-hook=" lib/concept_web/live/page_editor_live.ex~ shows BlockList, FormatToolbar, SlashMenu mounted. No orphan ~phx-hook="PageEditor"~ in this file (BUG-027 not yet — fine for this wave). - Reports any ~CustomEvent~ dispatched by a Lit component that has no listener in the hook layer. ** ITEM Wave 3 — Shell + lifecycle (mostly parallel; one serial chain on app.css) :PROPERTIES: :CUSTOM_ID: PLAN-005-concept-editor-and-shell-rescue-post-pla::wave-3 :END: Five items. BUG-019 + BUG-021 both touch =assets/css/app.css= → serialize. Others parallel. - [[id:BUG-019-authoverrides-module-is-empty-sign-in-re]] AuthOverrides + .ora-auth-* CSS layer - Files: =lib/concept_web/auth_overrides.ex=, =assets/css/app.css= (auth section), =test/concept_web/controllers/auth_controller_test.exs= (new or extend). - [[id:BUG-021-block-lock-indicators-not-rendered-from]] Lock indicators via presence metadata - Blockers: BUG-019 (app.css serialization). - Files: =lib/concept_web/live/page_editor_live.ex= (focus/blur/diff handlers), =lib/concept_web/components/block_render.ex= (data-locked-by + --lock-color), =assets/css/app.css= (verify lock-bar rule), =test/concept_web/live/lock_indicator_test.exs= (new). - [[id:BUG-020-onboarded-user-stranded-on-landing-no-po]] Workspace ~primary?~ attr + AfterSignIn redirect + HomeLive signed-in branch - Files: =lib/concept/accounts/workspace.ex=, =priv/repo/migrations/_add_primary_to_workspaces.exs= (new), =lib/concept/accounts/reactors/onboarding.ex=, =lib/concept/accounts.ex= (new helper), =lib/concept_web/live_user_auth.ex=, =lib/concept_web/router.ex=, =lib/concept_web/live/home_live.ex=, =test/concept_web/live/auth_flow_test.exs= (new). - [[id:BUG-022-pageheader-stale-on-remote-rename-missin]] PageHeader ~send_update~ on page_updated - Files: =lib/concept_web/live/workspace_live.ex= (handle_info), =test/concept_web/live/page_header_test.exs= (extend with multi-LV scenario). - [[id:BUG-023-empty-title-placeholder-broken-on-whites]] Trim title server + client - Files: =lib/concept/pages/changes/trim_title.ex= (new), =lib/concept/pages/page.ex= (wire change), =assets/js/hooks/content_editable.js= (trim), =test/concept/pages/page_title_test.exs= (new), =assets/js/hooks/__tests__/content_editable.test.js= (new). ** ITEM Wave 3.5 — Reviewer R3 :PROPERTIES: :CUSTOM_ID: PLAN-005-concept-editor-and-shell-rescue-post-pla::wave-3-review :END: Subagent: ~reviewer~. Checks: - ~mix precommit~ + vitest green. - Puppeteer: register a fresh user → confirm via test-mode magic link → assert URL is ~/w/~ + sidebar visible. - Puppeteer: screenshot /sign-in, /register, /reset → manually inspect parity vs ~04-workspace-shell.png~ baseline (Inter font, neutral palette, rounded inputs). - Puppeteer: two browser sessions on the same page; session-A focuses block X → session-B sees colored left bar on block X within ≤ 2s; session-A blurs → bar gone within ≤ 2s. - Puppeteer: two browser sessions; session-A renames page → session-B's

      updates within ≤ 2s; browser tab title also updates. - Puppeteer: enter " " (single space) as title → save → reload → empty + "Untitled" placeholder visible. - Reports any presence metadata field touched without authorization (privacy regression). ** ITEM Wave 4 — Cleanup + parity (parallel; disjoint) :PROPERTIES: :CUSTOM_ID: PLAN-005-concept-editor-and-shell-rescue-post-pla::wave-4 :END: - [[id:BUG-024-blockkeyboard-hook-is-dead-code-plan-002]] Delete dead =assets/js/hooks/block_keyboard.js= - [[id:BUG-025-homelive-missing-globalkeys-hook-cmd-k-s]] Mount GlobalKeys on HomeLive - [[id:BUG-027-pageeditor-container-phx-hook-attribute]] Drop orphan ~phx_hook: "PageEditor"~ from WorkspaceLive's =live_render= container option ** ITEM Wave 4.5 — Reviewer R4 :PROPERTIES: :CUSTOM_ID: PLAN-005-concept-editor-and-shell-rescue-post-pla::wave-4-review :END: Subagent: ~reviewer~. Checks: - ~mix precommit~ + vitest green. - ~rg -n "BlockKeyboard|block_keyboard" lib test assets~ returns zero hits. - ~rg -n 'phx_hook: "PageEditor"' lib~ returns zero hits. - Puppeteer: signed-in user on / → Cmd-K → redirect to /w (verifies BUG-025 wiring). - Reports any other orphan hook attributes (~rg 'phx-hook="(\w+)"' lib~ vs hook keys in =assets/js/hooks/index.js=). ** ITEM Wave 5 — Deferred feature :PROPERTIES: :CUSTOM_ID: PLAN-005-concept-editor-and-shell-rescue-post-pla::wave-5 :END: - [[id:FEAT-050-composite-blocks-table-columns-child-cel]] Composite blocks (table + columns) child cell creation flow ** ITEM Wave 5.5 — Reviewer R5 (final E2E) :PROPERTIES: :CUSTOM_ID: PLAN-005-concept-editor-and-shell-rescue-post-pla::wave-5-review :END: Subagent: ~reviewer~. Full user journey on a fresh DB: 1. Register new user → land on /w/. 2. Sidebar empty → click "+ New page". 3. New page renders with editable title + emoji button. 4. Click title → type "My first page" → blur → sidebar updates. 5. Click emoji → pick 📒 → header updates. 6. Click cover band → pick blue → header gets blue cover. 7. Click "+ Click to add your first block" → paragraph appears. 8. Type "Hello world". 9. Press Enter at end → new empty paragraph below; caret in it. 10. Type "/h1 Title" → wait — typo: type "/h1" actually selects heading_1 → press Enter → heading_1 block appears; typed content. Type "Big title". 11. ArrowUp → caret in "Hello world" paragraph at end. 12. Select "world" → click B in format toolbar → bolded. 13. Select "Hello" → click link → enter "https://elixir-lang.org" → Enter → linked. 14. Save (auto on blur). Reload the page → 12+13 persist. 15. Drag the handle of the heading block above the paragraph → reorder persists on reload. 16. Cmd-K → palette opens → type page title → Enter → navigates back to the page. 17. Open a second browser tab on same URL → presence avatar appears in PresenceBar in both tabs; renaming title from tab 1 reflects in tab 2 within 2s. 18. In tab 1 focus block A → tab 2 shows colored left-bar on block A. 19. Slash → Table → 2×2 picker → table appears with 4 cells; Tab from cell (1,1) focuses (1,2); type in each cell, reload, persists. 20. Sign out → /sign-in renders with .ora-auth-root visible. All 20 steps must pass. Any failure files a child BUG and the wave reports ~gate_failed~. * Verification (overall) - ~mix precommit~ green at every wave close. - ~cd assets && npx vitest run~ green at every wave close. - Reviewer puppeteer flow passes end-to-end at W5.5. - Test count target: +60–80 scenarios across 13 BUGs + 1 FEAT. - No file in =lib/= imports a hook by name string that isn't in =assets/js/hooks/index.js= (orphan-hook regression check, runs in CI). - No CustomEvent dispatched by an =ora-*= component without a listener somewhere in =assets/js/hooks/= (orphan-event regression check, vitest static scan). * Execution principles (recap, normative for every subagent) 1. NEVER ~git stash | checkout | reset | revert | clean -f~. Forward-fix only. 2. Each item touches the file set declared in its node. Reviewers cross-check. 3. Each wave closes with ~mix precommit~ green + a single commit per item. 4. Reviewer waves are read-only; they ~report_finding~ + file follow-ups, never edit. 5. Reviewer subagent ≠ implementer subagent for the same item. 6. Puppeteer is mandatory in every reviewer wave — server tests passing is not sufficient evidence. 7. Items declaring ~blockers~ within a wave serialize cleanly; without ~blockers~ they run in parallel. 8. Failing reviewer → ~gate_failed~ on the item + new child BUG; wave proceeds; next wave gated on overall green at close. * References - [[id:PLAN-001-notion-mvp-pixel-perfect]] — original Notion MVP plan (DONE; overstated) - [[id:PLAN-002-fix-editor-ux-edit-bars-enter-inserts-bl]] — first editor-fix plan (DONE; regressed on JS side) - [[id:PLAN-004-arcana-awareness-let-the-document-be-awa]] — Arcana/RAG plan; explicitly disjoint scope - =assets/js/hooks/SERVER_CONTRACT.md= — canonical event names/payloads; do not rename - AGENTS.md — Phoenix 1.8 / LiveView / HEEx mandates including ~current_scope~ and == contract - Frontend audit transcript: this session, agents 0-EditorBlockUX, 1-ShellPageHeaderNav, 2-PaletteAuthHome, 3-AIArcanaFrontendGaps (2026-05-16)

+| /Scope-drift reviewer/ | Each commit's diff stays within the item's declared ~filesDeps~. No accidental Arcana touches. No drive-by edits. Commit attribution accurate (file delta matches the message). |
+| /Build & test runner/ | ~mix compile --warnings-as-errors~ + ~mix test~ + ~cd assets && npx vitest run~ + ~mix precommit~. No new warnings. Test count matches claims. |
+| /Architecture reviewer/ | Implementation matches the architecture sketch in PLAN body. No surprising new modules. No future-Arcana coupling. |

defmodule Concept.Knowledge.Link do
  @moduledoc """
  User-authored block-to-block graph edges. Each Link row mirrors into
  `Arcana.Graph.Relationship` via an after_action change.
  """
  use Ash.Resource,
    otp_app: :concept,
    domain: Concept.Knowledge,
    data_layer: AshPostgres.DataLayer,
    authorizers: [Ash.Policy.Authorizer],
    extensions: [AshPaperTrail.Resource]

  postgres do
    table "knowledge_links"
    repo Concept.Repo

    references do
      reference :source_block, on_delete: :delete
      reference :target_block, on_delete: :delete
    end
  end

  multitenancy do
    strategy :attribute
    attribute :workspace_id
    global? false
  end

  paper_trail do
    primary_key_type :uuid
  end

  attributes do
    uuid_primary_key :id

    attribute :workspace_id, :uuid, allow_nil?: false, public?: true
    attribute :source_block_id, :uuid, allow_nil?: false, public?: true
    attribute :target_block_id, :uuid, allow_nil?: false, public?: true

    attribute :kind, :atom,
      allow_nil?: false,
      public?: true,
      constraints: [one_of: [:relates_to, :cites, :contradicts, :see_also]]

    attribute :note, :string, public?: true
    attribute :created_by_user_id, :uuid, allow_nil?: false, public?: true

    create_timestamp :inserted_at

before:
defmodule Concept.TestSupport.LLMStub do
  @moduledoc """
  Test-time HTTP stub for LLM calls (Gemini via ReqLLM via AshAI).

  Blessed Elixir pattern: ReqLLM forwards `req_http_options:` to `Req.new/1`,
  which honors `plug: {Req.Test, stub_name}`. Tests register canned responses
  with `Req.Test.stub/2`, dispatch the AshAI/ReqLLM action, and assert.

      iex> Concept.TestSupport.LLMStub.stub_text("Hello, world!")
      :ok
      iex> Concept.TestSupport.LLMStub.req_http_options()
      [plug: {Req.Test, Concept.TestSupport.LLMStub}]

  Place the returned options under `req_llm_opts:` in AshAi.ToolLoop calls,
  or pass directly to `ReqLLM.generate_text/3` as `req_http_options:`.

  ## Stubs available
  - `stub_text/2`        — plain (non-streaming) text reply
  - `stub_stream/2`      — streaming SSE chunks for a single text response
  - `stub_tool_call/2`   — assistant invokes a single tool, then returns text
  - `stub_embedding/1`   — embedding response (Gemini batchEmbedContents shape)
  - `stub_error/2`       — return an HTTP error status

  All stubs are scoped to the current test process via `Req.Test`.
  Multi-process scenarios (LiveView, Oban worker) should call
  `Req.Test.allow/3` to extend the stub to the worker pid.
  """

  @doc "Pass under `req_llm_opts:` or `req_http_options:` to route requests through Req.Test."
  @spec req_http_options() :: keyword()
  def req_http_options, do: [plug: {Req.Test, __MODULE__}]

  @doc "Same, wrapped for AshAi.ToolLoop convenience."
  @spec ash_ai_opts() :: keyword()
  def ash_ai_opts, do: [req_llm_opts: req_http_options()]

  # ── Non-streaming text reply ──────────────────────────────────────────
  @doc "Return a plain text completion (Gemini chat shape)."
  def stub_text(text, opts \\ []) do
    Req.Test.stub(__MODULE__, fn conn ->
      Req.Test.json(conn, gemini_chat_body(text, opts))
    end)
  end

  # ── Streaming text reply (SSE) ────────────────────────────────────────
  @doc """
  Return a streaming text completion as Gemini SSE chunks.

  `text` is split into ~4-char pieces and streamed as `text/event-stream`,
  ending with `data: [DONE]`.
  """
  def stub_stream(text, opts \\ []) do
    Req.Test.stub(__MODULE__, fn conn ->
      sse_body =
        text
        |> chunks_of(Keyword.get(opts, :chunk_size, 4))
        |> Enum.map(&gemini_stream_chunk/1)
        |> Enum.join()
        |> Kernel.<>(gemini_stream_done())

      conn
      |> Plug.Conn.put_resp_content_type("text/event-stream")
      |> Plug.Conn.send_resp(200, sse_body)
    end)
  end

  # ── Tool call ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  @doc """
  First call returns an assistant tool_call; on the *next* call, returns text.

  Wires the round-trip needed by `AshAi.ToolLoop.run/2`: model asks for a
  tool, runtime invokes it, runtime calls model again with the tool result,
  model returns final text.
  """
  def stub_tool_call(tool_name, args, opts \\ []) do
    final_text = Keyword.get(opts, :final_text, "Tool executed.")
    {:ok, agent} = Agent.start_link(fn -> :first end)

    Req.Test.stub(__MODULE__, fn conn ->
      case Agent.get_and_update(agent, fn s -> {s, :second} end) do
        :first  -> Req.Test.json(conn, gemini_tool_call_body(tool_name, args))
        :second -> Req.Test.json(conn, gemini_chat_body(final_text))
      end
    end)
  end

  # ── Embedding response ────────────────────────────────────────────────
  @doc "Return a batch embedding response with deterministic vectors per input."
  def stub_embedding(vectors_by_text \\ %{}) when is_map(vectors_by_text) do
    Req.Test.stub(__MODULE__, fn conn ->
      {:ok, body, conn} = Plug.Conn.read_body(conn)
      %{"requests" => reqs} = Jason.decode!(body)

      embeds =
        for %{"content" => %{"parts" => [%{"text" => text}]}} <- reqs do
          %{"values" => Map.get(vectors_by_text, text, default_vector(text))}
        end

      Req.Test.json(conn, %{"embeddings" => embeds})
    end)
  end

  # ── Error response ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  @doc "Return an HTTP error (e.g. 429 rate limit, 500 server)."
  def stub_error(status, body \\ %{"error" => %{"message" => "stubbed error"}}) do
    Req.Test.stub(__MODULE__, fn conn ->
      conn
      |> Plug.Conn.put_status(status)
      |> Req.Test.json(body)
    end)
  end

  # ── Internals ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  defp gemini_chat_body(text, _opts) do
    %{
      "candidates" => [
        %{
          "content" => %{"role" => "model", "parts" => [%{"text" => text}]},
          "finishReason" => "STOP",
          "index" => 0
        }
      ],
      "usageMetadata" => %{
        "promptTokenCount" => 12,
        "candidatesTokenCount" => max(1, div(String.length(text), 4)),
        "totalTokenCount" => 12 + max(1, div(String.length(text), 4))
      }
    }
  end

  defp gemini_tool_call_body(tool_name, args) do
    %{
      "candidates" => [
        %{
          "content" => %{
            "role" => "model",
            "parts" => [%{"functionCall" => %{"name" => tool_name, "args" => args}}]
          },
          "finishReason" => "STOP",
          "index" => 0
        }
      ],
      "usageMetadata" => %{
        "promptTokenCount" => 12,
        "candidatesTokenCount" => 4,
        "totalTokenCount" => 16
      }
    }
  end

  defp gemini_stream_chunk(text) do
    payload =
      Jason.encode!(%{
        "candidates" => [
          %{"content" => %{"role" => "model", "parts" => [%{"text" => text}]}, "index" => 0}
        ]
      })

    "data: " <> payload <> "\n\n"
  end

  defp gemini_stream_done, do: "data: [DONE]\n\n"

  defp chunks_of("", _), do: [""]

  defp chunks_of(string, size) do
    string
    |> String.graphemes()
    |> Enum.chunk_every(size)
    |> Enum.map(&Enum.join/1)
  end

  defp default_vector(text) do
    Concept.Knowledge.MockEmbedder.embed_batch([text], [])
    |> case do
      {:ok, [v]} -> v
      _ -> List.duplicate(0.0, 384)
    end
  end
end

defmodule Concept.Knowledge.ProfileBuilder do
  @moduledoc """
  Entry point for declaring profiles via the Knowledge profile DSL.

      defmodule MyApp.Profiles do
        use Concept.Knowledge.ProfileBuilder

        profile :fast do
          rewrite false
          search mode: :semantic, limit: 6
          rerank false
          answer model: "google:gemini-2.5-flash-lite"
          tools [:search_workspace]
        end
      end

  Introspection helpers (list/0, get/1, get!/1) are injected at compile time.
  """
  defmacro __using__(_opts) do
    quote do
      use Spark.Dsl,
        many_extension_kinds: [:extensions],
        default_extensions: [extensions: [unquote(Concept.Knowledge.ProfileDsl)]]

      @doc "List all profiles declared in this module."
      def list, do: Spark.Dsl.Extension.get_entities(__MODULE__, [:profiles])

      @doc "Get a profile by name; returns nil if not found."
      def get(name) when is_atom(name), do: Enum.find(list(), &(&1.name == name))

      @doc "Get a profile by name; raises ArgumentError if not found."
      def get!(name) when is_atom(name) do
        get(name) || raise ArgumentError, "unknown profile: #{inspect(name)}"
      end

      @doc "Profile name → keyword list consumable by Arcana.Pipeline."
      def to_pipeline_opts(name), do: name |> get!() |> Concept.Knowledge.Profile.to_pipeline_opts()

      @doc "Profile name → keyword list consumable by AshAi.ToolLoop."
      def to_ash_ai_opts(name), do: name |> get!() |> Concept.Knowledge.Profile.to_ash_ai_opts()
    end
  end
end
